title: Burtonia scabra
taxonomy: Burtonia scabra is a plant species in the Fabaceae family. It belongs to the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and phylogenetic relationships. Burtonia scabra is a member of the genus Burtonia, which includes several other plant species. The Fabaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families in the world.

intro: Burtonia scabra, also known as rough burtonia, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing, fever reduction, and protection against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Burtonia scabra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Burtonia scabra is often used to make tea infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
